text.title: Solo Deploy Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Solo Deploy Checklist methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every deploy artefact MUST reference a documented rollback path (link or inline script). Empty rollback_plan blocks the deploy.
rule.rationale: A deploy without a rollback is a one-way door; refuse to ship without the way back.

rule.statement: If the deploy includes a DB migration, a fresh backup (≤24h) MUST be taken AND the restore-drill verified within the last 90 days.
rule.rationale: Migration rollback often needs data restore; an unverified backup is no backup.

rule.statement: CI status MUST be green on the deploy commit. 'flaky test, ignored' is forbidden — either fix or quarantine first.
rule.rationale: Amber CI normalises broken windows; once 1 fails 'safely', 2 follow.

rule.statement: Every DeployChecklist names a single owner. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
No-go deploys after Friday 16:00 local time UNLESS owner is on-call through the weekend AND incident-response is reachable.
rule.rationale: Late-Friday deploys + Monday discovery is the canonical solo regression pattern.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-solo-deploy-checklist.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
